back_translation: Then Joseph commanded the steward of his house, saying, 'Fill the men's sacks with food as much as they can carry, and put each man's silver in the mouth of his sack.'
And put my cup, the silver one, in the mouth of the youngest one's sack, along with the silver for his grain.' And the steward did according to the word that Joseph had spoken.
When morning was light, the men were sent on their way — they and their donkeys.
They had not gone far from the city when Joseph said to the steward of his house, 'Arise, pursue the men, and when you overtake them, say to them: Why have you repaid evil for good?'
Is this not the cup my master drinks from and that he uses for divination? You have done evil in what you have done.'
When the steward overtook them, he spoke these words to them.
They answered him, 'Why does my lord speak such words? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing.'
Behold, the silver we found in the mouths of our sacks we returned to you from the land of Canaan; how then would we steal silver or gold from your lord's house?
If any of your servants is found with the cup, let him die, and we also will be slaves of my lord.'
He said, 'Now also let it be according to your words. The one with whom it is found shall be my slave, and the rest shall be free of guilt.'
Then each one quickly lowered his sack to the ground and opened his sack.
The steward searched, beginning with the eldest and ending with the youngest, and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
Then they tore their clothes, each loaded his donkey, and returned to the city.
Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house. Joseph was still there, and they fell down before him on the ground.
Joseph said to them, 'What deed is this that you have done? Did you not know that a man like me can divine accurately?'

Judah said, 'What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? How shall we justify ourselves? God has revealed the iniquity of your servants. Behold, we are slaves of my lord — both we and the one in whose hand the cup was found.'
But Joseph said, 'Far be it from me to do this. The one in whose hand the cup was found, he shall be my slave, and as for you, go up in peace to your father.'
Then Judah came near to Joseph and said, 'Please, my lord, let your servant speak a word in the ears of my lord, and let your anger not burn against your servant, for you are like Pharaoh.'
My lord asked his servants, 'Have you a father or a brother?'
We answered my lord, 'We have an old father and a young child of his old age, the youngest. His brother is dead, and he alone remains of his mother, and his father loves him.'
Then you commanded your servants, 'Bring him down to me that I may set my eye upon him.'
We said to my lord, 'The boy cannot leave his father; if he leaves his father, his father will die.'
You said to your servants, 'If your youngest brother does not come down with you, you shall not see my face again.'
When we went up to your servant our father, we told him the words of my lord.
Our father said, 'Return, buy us a little food.'
We answered, 'We cannot go down. Only if our youngest brother is with us will we go down, for we cannot see the face of the man if our youngest brother is not with us.'
Your servant our father said to us, 'You know that my wife bore me two sons.'
One went out from me, and I said, 'Surely he has been torn to pieces,' and I have not seen him until now.
If you take this one also from me, and harm comes to him, you will bring my gray hair down to Sheol in sorrow.'
Now when I return to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us — and his soul is bound up with the boy's soul —
when he sees that the boy is not there, he will die. Your servants will bring the gray hair of your servant our father down to Sheol in sorrow.
For your servant guaranteed the boy to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, I will bear the guilt against my father forever.'
Now therefore, please let your servant remain in place of the boy as a slave to my lord, and let the boy go up with his brothers.
For how can I go up to my father if the boy is not with me? Lest I see the evil that would find my father.
